title: 2'-Mercapto Maleianilic Acid as a New Gravimatric Reagent for the Determination and Separation of Zirconium(IV) and Thorium(IV)
author: NEPAL SINGH
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Hindu College, Moradabad 

  Manuscript received 11 May 1982, revised 27 April 1983, accepted 1 September 1983  

 ORGANIC acids and their derivatives have been investigated by several workers for the estimation of zirconium 1 - 6  and thorium 7 -11  In continuation of our previous work 12 , we report the use of 2'-mercapto maleianilic acid as a new gravimatric reagent for the determination of Zr(IV) and Th(IV) and their seperation from binary mixture by using a mixture of citrate and tartrate (1 : 1 molar ratio) as masking agent for zirconium(IV) ion. Ammonium nitrate has been used as a coagulating agent for better crystallization.

Ammonium nitrate has been used as a coagulating agent for better crystallization.
